name: Critical Hit
description: A high-impact hit result caused by chance, precision, vulnerability, or build rules.
design_notes: Critical hits can reward precision, build investment, or opportunistic timing. The design should separate random critical chance from deterministic weak-point or backstab rules, because each creates different player expectations and balance pressures.
design_purpose: Create moments of heightened payoff from precision, build investment, or vulnerability.
Differentiate weapons, stats, enemy weak points, and combat styles.
Reward players for aiming, timing, positioning, or stacking relevant modifiers.
player_fantasy: The player feels they found the perfect opening or built toward explosive damage.

parameters.name: critical_chance_percent
parameters.description: Probability that an eligible hit becomes critical.

parameters.name: critical_damage_multiplier
parameters.description: Damage multiplier applied to critical hits.

parameters.name: critical_immunity_tag_ids
parameters.description: Tags that prevent critical hits on certain targets.

parameters.name: critical_feedback_priority
parameters.description: Priority used for hit markers, numbers, audio, and camera response.

edge_cases: Damage over time, area attacks, summons, and reflected projectiles need eligibility rules.
Critical chance above 100% can convert to extra damage, multi-crit tiers, or be clamped.
Guaranteed crit buffs must define whether they are consumed on blocked, immune, or missed attacks.
Networked random crits need deterministic server authority and client feedback correction.
common_bugs: Critical multiplier applies before and after armor reduction unintentionally.
Weak-point hits also roll random critical and double dip without design approval.
UI shows critical feedback for zero-damage immune hits.
Client predicts critical hits with a different random seed than the server.
balancing_notes: Random crits add excitement but can undermine fairness in competitive or precision-heavy games.
Deterministic weak-point crits reward mastery and can be balanced through aiming difficulty and exposure windows.
accessibility_notes: Use shape, text, sound, or hit-marker variation in addition to color for critical feedback.
Avoid making tiny weak points mandatory without aim assists, alternate strategies, or readable target presentation.

implementation_notes.unity: Model combat timing and hit resolution as data-owned state, drive animation from that state, and apply damage through one authoritative resolver rather than animation-only callbacks.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep combat windows on the actor state, evaluate Area2D/Area3D or shape queries during physics ticks, and emit presentation signals after authoritative damage resolution.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Gameplay Ability System, Actor Components, or replicated combat state for timing and damage authority, with montage notifies only marking windows or presentation events.
implementation_notes.web: Resolve combat in the fixed simulation step from actor state, hit volumes, and attack metadata, then queue UI/audio/camera events from the confirmed result.

example_games.title: Team Fortress 2
example_games.note: Uses critical hit feedback as a distinct combat result.

example_games.title: Monster Hunter: World
example_games.note: Rewards attacking weak zones with stronger damage outcomes.
